user_message: I have this email thread from Quantum Analytics that needs prep a brief. Renewal conversation is tomorrow.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 13:38 EST
Subject: Re: Re: competitive positioning Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with competitive positioning. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
Date: 2024-02-19 09:09 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Quantum Analytics

Our team encountered some challenges with competitive positioning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Robert Foster <robert.foster@quantumanaly.com>
To: Marcus Williams <marcus.williams@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 16:55 EST
Subject: URGENT: competitive positioning Issue at Quantum Analytics

Just checking in on the competitive positioning proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

signals.detail: Signal detected for Quantum Analytics: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

handoffs.condition: MSX records need dark-period hygiene catchup

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF41-D trap (Response draft over-eager / 'where have you been' tone); produces complete Context reconstruction, re-qualification assessment, response draft (appropriate tone), stakeholder
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF41-D failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF41-D failure mode (Response draft over-eager / 'where have you been' tone)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct competitive positioning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF41-D -- Response draft over-eager / 'where have you been' tone
